channel.title: Woodmont Industrial News Feed
channel.description: Daily industrial and CRE updates across key regions

item.title: Industrial Market Surges as Varda Space Expands in El Segundo
item.description: El Segundo — Varda Space expands its presence, contributing to a surge in the industrial market.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News

item.title: High-demand Industrial Sites and Energy Strategy
item.description: From advanced manufacturing to hyperscale data centers, many modern industrial developments are requiring unprecedented levels of energy – and the demand is growing exponentially. A panel of experts at I.CON West this week discussed the challenges in locating and securing power, as well as strategies to overcome grid constraints, improve energy efficiency and use artificial intelligence to model and plan power needs.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: Industrial AI and Ops: Clean Data, Taller Boxes, Smarter Decisions
item.description: Automation has been quietly reshaping industrial buildings for years, but AI still feels like the new kid in the warehouse. The discussion brought together perspectives from development, automation design and portfolio operations.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Kathryn Atkins

item.title: Crow Holdings, partners sell 1 million sq. ft. Exit 8 warehouse to BGO, in deal arranged by JLL
item.description: A team led by Crow Holdings Development has sold nearly 1 million square feet of new logistics space in Millstone, in a deal with the global real estate investment manager BGO.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: MP Materials Expands DFW Footprint With 120-Acre, $1.25B Project
item.description: A $1.25B magnet manufacturing campus is headed to Northlake, deepening DFW’s role in the nation’s defense supply chain.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: CRE Daily

item.title: Logistics Property Co. Secures 937,000 Square Foot Warehouse Lease At South Penn Logistics Center - Traded
item.description: Logistics Property Co. Secures 937,000 Square Foot Warehouse Lease At South Penn Logistics Center Traded
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News

item.title: Power, AI and Risk: What’s Next for Industrial Real Estate in the West
item.description: Industrial real estate across the Western U.S. is entering a new phase shaped by technology, infrastructure constraints and evolving supply chain strategies. While the sector remains fundamentally strong, the drivers of demand – and the challenges of delivering new space – are shifting.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Kathryn Atkins

item.title: Warehouse Service Provider Inks 937K SF Lease In Suburban Philly
item.description: A massive warehouse overlooking the Delaware River in Bucks County has been fully leased by a new tenant.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: noah.zucker@bisnow.com

item.title: Risk, Reality and Recovery: Industrial Leaders on What Comes Next
item.description: "Industrial capital markets have clearly moved out of the zero cap rate environment that defined the last cycle. Today, we're operating in a more disciplined market. Capital costs are higher. Leasing fundamentals have moderated, and investors are being forced to price risk much more carefully,” said Andrew Briner, executive managing director, Newmark, setting the scene for a panel discussion on industrial capital markets at NAIOP’s I.CON West this week.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Brielle Scott

item.title: Najarian Associates marketing, communications director marks milestone with consulting firm
item.description: Najarian Associates is marking the 20th anniversary of Ohannes Najarian, a second-generation member of its leadership team and a key figure in its business development efforts.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Seagis signs two new leases totaling 25,000 sq. ft. at updated industrial building in Carlstadt
item.description: Seagis Property Group has filled some 25,000 square feet of industrial space in the Meadowlands with new leases to a logistics service provider and a national stone company.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: NAI Hanson welcomes Godau as vice president in Parsippany office
item.description: Brian Godau has joined NAI James E. Hanson, where the veteran broker will serve the firm’s roster of owner, occupier and institutional clients.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Manufacturer buys 50,000 sq. ft. Edison warehouse for $18 million, NAIDB says
item.description: A developer has sold more than 50,000 square feet of modern industrial space in Edison for $18.2 million, brokers with NAI DiLeo-Bram & Co. announced Tuesday.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Kroger taps inventory drones for cold chain distribution operations
item.description: The system autonomously scans pallet locations across ambient and freezer zones, enabling weekly, facility-wide inventory visibility.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Kelly Stroh

item.title: Akston Establishes Caddo Parish, Louisiana, Manufacturing Operations
item.description: Akston, a biotechnology company focused on animal health, has established manufacturing operations in Shreveport, Louisiana. The $7 million project is expected to create 69 jobs in Caddo Parish.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Area Development Master

item.title: ‘It’s not just all the big companies’: Warehouse robotics use expands
item.description: As-a-service models and success stories from peers are pushing SMEs to invest in automation technology within their supply chains alongside giants like Walmart and Amazon.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: S.L. Fuller

item.title: SeaVee Boats sells Medley HQ for $51M in leaseback deal
item.description: SeaVee Boats will stay anchored in Medley after selling its global headquarters to Sagard Real Estate for $51 million. An affiliate of Denver-based Sagard, led by Mark Bigarel, acquired the boat showroom and manufacturing complex at 11081 Northwest South River Drive, records and real estate database Vizzda show. The buyer financed the purchase with a $30.6 million loan from BMO Bank. The transaction breaks down to $273 per square foot for three warehouses spanning 186,400 square feet. SeaVee, led by Moises Rodriguez, Ralph Torres and Ariel Pared, signed a 10-year lease with four five-year renewal options, Vizzda states. Avison Young’s […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Francisco Alvarado

item.title: Johnson & Johnson Plans Montgomery County, Pennsylvania, Manufacturing Operations
item.description: Global health care company Johnson & Johnson plans to establish operations in Montgomery County, Pennsylvania. The approximately $1 billion project is expected to create more than 500 jobs over twelve years.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Area Development Master

item.title: Bridge lands $57M construction loan for Doral office-to-industrial redev
item.description: Bridge Industrial scored a $56.7 million construction loan to redevelop a Doral corporate campus into warehouses. The deal comes as South Florida developers are seizing on suburban office complexes with plans to redevelop the sites. Aging offices in the western part of South Florida have struggled due to remote work and from the competition created by coastal areas with newer office stock. Chicago-based Bridge plans to redevelop the 16-acre site at 7775 Northwest 48th Street with a pair of warehouses spanning a combined 268,700 square feet, according to records. The firm recorded a notice of construction commencement. Associated Bank provided […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Lidia Dinkova

item.title: Vantage Commercial lists legacy yacht brand, manufacturing facility in Burlington County
item.description: The owners of a historic yacht manufacturer are seeking a buyer for the brand and related assets, including a waterfront facility in Burlington County, in a new listing by Vantage Commercial.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Growing Wages and CRE Impacts
item.description: According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics’ January 2026 labor report, average hourly earnings increased by 3.7% year over year. With that statistic in hand, Marcus & Millichap’s Senior Vice President, John Chang, explained how that wage increase relates to commercial real estate. In a recently released video, “How Wage Growth is Reinforcing CRE,” Chang ... The post Growing Wages and CRE Impacts appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Amy Wolff Sorter

item.title: Fanatics Expands West Village Lease to Entire Building
item.description: Fanatics Holdings, a global sports platform and leading provider of licensed sports merchandise, has signed a long-term lease to become the sole tenant at middle-market real estate private equity firm Meadow Partners’ 95 Morton St. in the West Village. Fanatics has leased approximately half of the property since 2022 and will now occupy 100% of the 210,000‑square‑foot Class-A ... The post Fanatics Expands West Village Lease to Entire Building appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: ALAN leader: lessons learned from nearly 20 years of crisis response
item.description: Kathy Fulton, who stepped into the role of executive director at American Logistics Aid Network after a tragedy, emphasizes the importance of embracing uncertainty.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Kelly Stroh

item.title: Wellington rejects billionaire Frank McCourt’s polo field redevelopment plans
item.description: Billionaire developer Frank McCourt’s plans with Discovery Land Company to build 27 homes and a country club on the Isla Carroll Polo field were shot down by Wellington village officials in a late-night vote that followed hours of testimony and discussion. Only Vice Mayor Tanya Siskind supported rezoning 79 acres at 3665 120th Avenue for McCourt Partners and Discovery Land to develop a horse-friendly subdivision. Along with the homes, the developers envision an 8-acre private club for 325 families, along with a 10-stall barn and equestrian complex, bridle paths and a polo field used by the United States Polo Association. […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Erik Bojnansky

item.title: NY brokerage plants flag in Wynwood, bets on “much bigger” Miami in-migration
item.description: A boutique New York brokerage planted a flag in Wynwood, betting Miami and South Florida as a whole are poised for a second, even bigger wave of out-of-state in-migration, The Real Deal has learned. Modern Spaces, led by Eric Benaim and based in Long Island City, opened an office this month at the Wyncatcher I building at 2143 Northwest First Avenue, according to the brokerage’s news release and Benaim. It leased less than 1,000 square feet for five agents, with plans to expand and bring its headcount to about 25 people by year’s end, Benaim said. It marks Modern Spaces’ […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Lidia Dinkova

item.title: Gosin Shakes Off AI Fears As Newmark Profit Jumps 41% Behind Surging Sales
item.description: Newmark CEO Barry Gosin shrugged off the artificial intelligence fears that have plagued other brokerages as his company posted record quarterly and full-year revenue and quarterly net income up more than 40% year-over-year. Gosin played offense against...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: bianca.barragan@bisnow.com

item.title: Why the U.S. 10-Year Yield Keeps Snapping Back to “Neutral”
item.description: Executive Summary The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield has spent most of the past three years oscillating between 3.75% and 4.50%, with a rolling two-year average near 4.2%. That level increasingly represents market “neutral” — consistent with a fed funds rate just above 3%, inflation near 2.5%, and nominal growth expectations around 4%. The Case for Neutral Despite ... The post Why the U.S. 10-Year Yield Keeps Snapping Back to “Neutral” appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joe Palmisano

item.title: A guiding force: Why time with Newark Alliance will loom large for new EDA chief Weiss
item.description: Evan Weiss had his reasons for leaving state government in 2021. One of them, he recalls, was “because I started to feel so removed” from the constituents who were affected by the sweeping policies and billions in funds he helped deploy as a top adviser to Gov. Phil Murphy. He rediscovered that connection as head of the nonprofit Newark Alliance, a role he held for four years — and one that will loom large as he returns to Trenton as CEO of the state Economic Development Authority.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Hormel taps Hershey veteran as chief supply chain officer
item.description: Will Bonifant will oversee the branded food company’s global supply chain, including manufacturing, procurement, and logistics, effective March 9.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Antone Gonsalves

item.title: GreenPoint Partners Nabs $300M Loan for Buy of Transport Infrastructure Portfolio
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: GlobeSt

item.title: February 2026 Industrial Report: Turbulence Builds in Port Markets

item.author: Lucian Alixandrescu

item.title: CoStar CEO Andy Florance Touts AI Push As Stock Hits 7-Year Low
item.description: Real estate data giant CoStar Group is positioning itself as a winner in the artificial intelligence-driven disruption to the industry, but thus far, Wall Street is treating it as a loser.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: jon.banister@bisnow.com

item.title: PREMIER Breaks Ground on Linx Global Manufacturing Facility in Niles
item.description: PREMIER Design + Build Group has begun construction on a new Linx Global Manufacturing facility in Niles, Illinois. The 82,125-square-foot building, with an additional 18,000-square-foot production mezzanine, will feature a modern office environment alongside a cutting-edge factory. Linx is a global product development and contract manufacturing company, and the new facility will expand Linx’s R&D ... The post PREMIER Breaks Ground on Linx Global Manufacturing Facility in Niles appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Availabilities — Properties for Sale or Lease
item.author: Jasmine Kilman

item.title: BRC: Industrial users ink deals in Linden, Kearny as small-bay and IOS demand continues
item.description: A global freight forwarding and logistics firm has signed a 20,000-square-foot industrial lease in Linden, in one of several recent transactions by Bussel Realty Corp.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Swift Creek Partners Spends $98.45M on Orlando Warehouse Portfolio
item.description: Swift Creek Partners picked up a four-building, 712,000 square foot industrial asset for $98.45 million. Link Logistics was the seller. The four buildings at the Crossroads Distribution Center are fully leased and include 1151 Gills Drive, a 252,000-square-foot distribution center; 1125 Gills Drive, a 152,240-square-foot building; 1091 Gills Drive, a 235,760-square-foot property; and 1075 Gills ... The post Swift Creek Partners Spends $98.45M on Orlando Warehouse Portfolio appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mike Boyd

item.title: Columbia Bank debuts in Rutherford with 2,300 sq. ft. lease arranged by Goldstein Group
item.description: Columbia Bank has opened a new branch in downtown Rutherford, where it occupies some 2,300 square feet that it secured with the help of The Goldstein Group.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: NAI Hanson brokers sale of 153,000 sq. ft. shallow-bay portfolio in Westchester County
item.description: An investment firm has purchased more than 153,000 square feet of shallow-bay industrial space in New York’s Westchester County, in a deal arranged by NAI James E. Hanson.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Weida Freight Systems Leases 20000 Square Feet Industrial Space In Linden From CHS Development - Traded

item.author: Google News Traded.co

item.title: JLL Adds Scott Greenwood on East Bay Agency Leasing Team
item.description: Scott Greenwood has joined JLL as managing director in its East Bay office. A 20-year commercial real estate veteran, Greenwood will focus on agency leasing across the East Bay and Oakland markets. Over the course of his career, Greenwood has completed more than four million square feet in leasing transactions and more than $800 million ... The post JLL Adds Scott Greenwood on East Bay Agency Leasing Team appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: White Paper: Q1 2026 Shipper Rate Report
item.description: The Shipper Rate Report — presented in partnership with Trimble — is a quarterly publication using SONAR datasets to create the most in-depth rate and demand outlook for shippers. The insights within this paper are curated by the market experts at FreightWaves and backed by the proprietary data and analytics housed in SONAR’s platform. Key areas of […] The post White Paper: Q1 2026 Shipper Rate Report appeared first on FreightWaves.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: FreightWaves Staff

item.title: Walmart: Supply chain spending set to ‘peak’ next 2 years, CEO says
item.description: The retail giant is continuing to automate regional distribution centers throughout the U.S. and starting to deploy technological improvements internationally.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Max Garland

item.title: AI Anxiety Has Found Its Way to Real-Estate Brokers
item.description: A selloff in commercial broker shares reflects investor fears that artificial intelligence will erode the industry’s fees. Executives say the human touch still matters.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Peter Grant

item.title: Woodmont sells new 54,000 sq. ft. warehouse in Woodbridge to Sagard Real Estate
item.description: Woodmont Industrial Partners has sold more than 54,000 square feet of newly built industrial space in Woodbridge to Sagard Real Estate, in a deal arranged by Newmark.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: CBRE: Ridgecut Road snags acquisition loan for Kenilworth industrial outdoor storage site
item.description: Ridgecut Road has secured acquisition financing for a roughly two-acre industrial outdoor storage property in Kenilworth, according to a debt placement team with CBRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: ICE's $38B Warehouse Buying Spree Enriches Developers As Outcry Grows
item.description: The eternal question of real estate investing — to sell or not to sell? — has perhaps never been harder to answer for owners of empty U.S. warehouses. The federal government plans to spend $38.3B to acquire industrial buildings and convert them...
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: ethan.rothstein@bisnow.com

item.title: ESRT Taps Newmark to Lease Up 130 Mercer Offices in SoHo
item.description: Newmark has been appointed exclusive leasing agent for 130 Mercer, the Scholastic Building in SoHo, on behalf of the new owner, Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT), which paid $386 million to acquire it in a late-2025 sale-leaseback. President, New York Tri-State Region David Falk, vice chairman Peter Shimkin, executive managing director Daniel Levine and director Dylan Weisman ... The post ESRT Taps Newmark to Lease Up 130 Mercer Offices in SoHo appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: Metropolitan Realty Refis Last-Mile Distribution Facility for $33M
item.description: Metropolitan Realty Associates LLC (MRA), led by CEO and founder Joseph Farkas, has refinanced a 315,000-square-foot, single story, last-mile distribution facility at 456 Sullivan Ave., S. Windsor, CT. Webster Bank closed the loan for $33,200,000. The loan paid off the existing mortgage held by Bank of America from the time of acquisition. MRA originally acquired the ... The post Metropolitan Realty Refis Last-Mile Distribution Facility for $33M appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: Industrial heir sells lakefront Palm Beach home for $57M
item.description: Ambrose K. Monell, the grandson of a Gilded Age industrialist, sold his lakefront Palm Beach home for $57 million in an off-market deal, marking another high-dollar transaction during the town’s peak real estate season. Records show he and his wife, Lili Monell, sold the house at 635 Crest Road to a trust named for the address and managed by prominent wealth management attorney Cameron Casey. Gary Pohrer with Serhant represented the buyer’s side. Monell’s grandfather and namesake was the nickel magnate Ambrose Monell. He became Carnegie’s chief metallurgical engineer before being named president of the International Nickel Company. He died […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Kate Hinsche

item.title: 1885 Swarthmore Acquires Industrial Property In Lakewood For $7.15M With Broker Dave Lunczer - Traded
item.description: 1885 Swarthmore Acquires Industrial Property In Lakewood For $7.15M With Broker Dave Lunczer Traded
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Google News Traded.co Availabilities

item.title: Temple Battery Maker Eyeing $110M Expansion
item.description: East Penn Manufacturing Co. has been producing batteries and energy storage systems in Temple since 2022. And now, the company is looking at a $110 million expansion. The company has hit up the Temple City Council for a tax abatement agreement to expand operations at 5101 Wendland Drive. It’s 393,000-square-foot battery finishing and distribution center ... The post Temple Battery Maker Eyeing $110M Expansion appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mike Boyd

item.title: Stonemont, Iron Point Partners Fully Lease Topline Logistics Center in Ocala, Florida
item.description: OCALA, FLA. — Atlanta-based private real estate investment firm Stonemont and private equity firm Iron Point Partners have signed an undisclosed tenant to a full-building lease at Topline Logistics Center, a 457,217-square-foot industrial facility located directly off I-75 in Ocala. Jared Bonshire, David Perez, Taylor Zambito and Ryan Hubbard of Cushman & Wakefield represented Stonemont in the lease negotiations. Situated on approximately 35 acres in Central Florida, the cross-dock facility features 36-foot clear heights, 110 trailer parking spaces, 247 car parking spaces, four drive-in doors and a 185-foot truck court. The post Stonemont, Iron Point Partners Fully Lease Topline Logistics Center in Ocala, Florida appeared first on REBusinessOnline.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Abby Cox

item.title: CIP Pays $51.6 Million for Jax Warehouses
item.description: CIP Real Estate secured $44.6 million in acquisition financing from New York Life for the JAX Airport Logistics Center in Jacksonville, Florida. CIP recently acquired the property directly from its developer, Pan American Co., for $51.6 million. Cushman & Wakefield’s Jason Hochman and Ron Granite represented CIP Real Estate in its transaction with New York ... The post CIP Pays $51.6 Million for Jax Warehouses appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mike Boyd

item.title: Walmart e-commerce sales top $150B for first time
item.description: Walmart’s e-commerce business, supported by its store-fulfillment and delivery services, grew 27% in the fourth quarter and has turned profitable. The post Walmart e-commerce sales top $150B for first time appeared first on FreightWaves.

item.author: Eric Kulisch

item.title: Hesperia Industrial Secures First Lien Construction Financing
item.description: JLL Capital Markets arranged $33.8 million in first lien construction financing for 10400 Amargosa Rd., a to-be-built, Class A industrial facility in Hesperia. The Covington Group will develop the project on behalf of owner/user Pixior Group. JLL represented the borrower in securing the five-year, fixed-rate loan through TDA Investment Group, on behalf of its pension client. The JLL ... The post Hesperia Industrial Secures First Lien Construction Financing appeared first on Connect CRE.

item.author: ryan.wangman@bisnow.com

item.title: 'Somebody Has to Execute The Deal': JLL CEO Dismisses AI Fears, Promises Stock Buybacks
item.description: JLL CEO Christian Ulbrich isn’t particularly worried about artificial intelligence derailing the brokerage’s growth. “At the end of the day, somebody has to execute the deal,” Ulbrich told analysts on the firm’s fourth-quarter earnings call...

item.author: matthew.wasielewski@bisnow.com

item.title: March lands full-building tenant, refinances new 70,000 sq. ft. warehouse in Wayne

item.title: .Chick-fil-A Supply Plans Winter Haven, Florida, Logistics-Distribution Operations

item.title: Regeneron taps JLL to market ex-Avon research campus in Suffern for sale, lease

item.title: Rail freight outlook waits for improved indicators

item.title: Ares, Makarora Tap Surging CMBS Market For $1.5B Plymouth Portfolio Refi
item.description: Weeks after acquiring Plymouth Industrial REIT in a $2.1B take-private deal, Ares Alternative Credit and Makarora Management are lining up a $1.46B CMBS refinance of the acquisition debt. Citi Real Estate, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are slated...
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: ryan.wangman@bisnow.com

item.title: The State of Women in Commercial Real Estate: Key Takeaways from the 2025 CREW Network Study
item.description: The commercial real estate industry continues to evolve in response to economic cycles, shifting workplace expectations, and the long-term effects of the COVID19 pandemic. Understanding how these changes are shaping talent, leadership and compensation is essential for firms competing in today’s market. The 2025 CREW Network benchmark study offers valuable insight into these dynamics, with a particular focus on women in the industry.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Marie Ruff

item.title: Foundry Starts Work on 485K-SF Pompano Beach Warehouse Project
item.description: Foundry Commercial, in partnership with Wheelock Street Capital, broke ground on Festival Logistics Park, a 485,000-square-foot industrial campus in Pompano Beach, Fla. Commercial Edge reports the team inked a $78 million construction loan originated by The Hartford Financial Services Group. Foundry Commercial purchased the site from IMC Equity Group for $66 million in September last year. ... The post Foundry Starts Work on 485K-SF Pompano Beach Warehouse Project appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mike Boyd

item.title: Arrow: Owner of 264,000 sq. ft. North Brunswick warehouse inks $34 million permanent loan
item.description: A developer has secured a $33.5 million permanent loan for some 264,000 square feet of industrial space in North Brunswick, in a deal arranged by Arrow Real Estate Advisors.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Bank OZK Furnishes Construction Loan for Perris Logistics Facility
item.description: JLL Capital Markets recently arranged $65.1 million in senior construction financing for Ellis Avenue Logistics Center, a 631,011-square-foot Class A industrial facility under development in Perris. Senior managing director Greg Brown, associate Allie Black and senior analyst Nick Englhard worked on behalf of the undisclosed borrower to secure a floating-rate, five-year loan, including extensions, through Bank OZK. “Our process ... The post Bank OZK Furnishes Construction Loan for Perris Logistics Facility appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: Morgan Properties Announces Co-CEOs, Executive Leader Changes
item.description: Morgan Properties announced the appointment of Jonathan and Jason Morgan as co-Chief Executive Officers, formalizing the leadership roles they have long held in guiding the company’s strategy, investments and operations. Along with the new co-CEOs, Morgan Properties has elevated seven leaders to C-suite positions, including the appointment of Greg Curci as the company’s first Chief ... The post Morgan Properties Announces Co-CEOs, Executive Leader Changes appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Jasmine Kilman

item.title: Jills put jewels in Compass’ South Florida crown as Coldwell’s No. 1 team
item.description: Coldwell Banker Realty again named Miami’s Jills Zeder Group its top team in the nation, with $1.9 billion in sales last year. The team, led by Jill Hertzberg, Jill Eber and Judy Zeder has been the big dog Coldwell team nationally for seven years running now. Since the Compass-Anywhere Real Estate merger, the group is now Robert Reffkin’s crown jewel in the South Florida market. The Jills far outpaced Compass’ other top teams with $1.4 billion in on-market sales, The Real Deal’s 2025 Miami-Dade County broker ranking found. The group out-performed the highest ranking Compass teams, including Chad Carroll’s the […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Kate Hinsche

item.title: Industrial Upgrades Fuel Demand for Taller Warehouses
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: CRE Daily

item.title: Bridge Industrial snags $86 million bridge loan for new 356,000 sq. ft. Bayonne warehouse
item.description: Bridge Industrial has landed more than $86 million in financing for some 356,000 square feet of newly built warehouse space in Bayonne, in a transaction arranged by JLL.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Blackstone Mortgage Rebounds as It Rebalances Away From Office
item.description: After resolving $2.3B in troubled loans, BXMT is refocusing on apartments and warehouses.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: CRE Daily

item.title: Chance Horky And Harrison Sheinberg Acquire Industrial Property From Alton Butler For $5.25M - Traded
item.description: Chance Horky And Harrison Sheinberg Acquire Industrial Property From Alton Butler For $5.25M Traded
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases

item.title: Private Investor Snags Costa Mesa Offices for $24M
item.description: Newmark arranged the $24.4-million sale of 1901 Newport Blvd., a 134,387-square-foot, three-story office property located in Costa Mesa. Buyer and seller were not identified. Vice chairman Paul Jones, executive managing director Ryan Plummer and senior managing director Brandon White, in collaboration with co-head, U.S. Capital Markets Kevin Shannon and vice chairman Ken White, represented the ... The post Private Investor Snags Costa Mesa Offices for $24M appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: Dalfen notches 30% gain in South Florida industrial sale as market rebounds
item.description: Dalfen Industrial cashed out of a Palm Beach County warehouse complex for $79.6 million, marking a 31.4 percent gain from its 2021 price as the market rebounds. An entity tied to Dallas-based Dalfen Industrial and Goldman Sachs sold the Turnpike Logistics Center at 1673 and 1715 Meathe Drive in an unincorporated area of the county to Boston-based Cabot, according to records and real estate database Vizzda. The price amounts to roughly $251 per square foot. Dalfen paid $60.6 million, or about $191 per square foot, for the complex in 2021, when it was just completed. Billionaire developer and investor Jeff […] This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Lidia Dinkova

item.title: Q4 Caps Strong 2025 for Net-Lease Investment Sales
item.description: U.S. net‑lease investment accelerated in the fourth quarter of 2025, rising 38% from Q3 and 13% year-over-year, CBRE reported Thursday. Supported by steady cap rates and a widening yield spread, the strong Q4 performance contributed to a 16% increase in full‑year 2025 activity, bringing the annual total to $51.4 billion. Industrial & logistics continued to account for ... The post Q4 Caps Strong 2025 for Net-Lease Investment Sales appeared first on Connect CRE.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Paul Bubny

item.title: Blackstone Names Ex-Lego Family Money Manager as Senior Adviser
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has named Thomas Schleicher, who used to manage money for the billionaire owners of Lego A/S, as a senior adviser for the Nordic region.
item.category: People News — Personnel Moves in CRE
item.author: Swetha Gopinath

item.title: As Older Industrial Buildings Bleed Occupancy, Owners Seek To Raise The Roof
item.description: Tens of millions of square feet of warehouses built as recently as the first decade of the 21st century are no longer up to snuff. Occupiers are increasingly dependent on robotics and automation, a trend that has made higher ceilings heights a top priority...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: noah.zucker@bisnow.com

item.title: Yard management technology moves out of the shadows as supply chains push for end-to-end visibility
item.description: While supply chains have poured investment into transportation and warehouse technology, yard and dock operations remain one of the most manual and least visible parts of the network, a gap that is increasingly difficult to manage as volumes fluctuate and labor tightens. While the C3 State of Yard and Dock Management 2026 survey is underway, […] The post Yard management technology moves out of the shadows as supply chains push for end-to-end visibility appeared first on FreightWaves.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Mary O'Connell

item.title: PSE&G buys Hillside industrial site for $4.4 million, eyeing substation expansion
item.description: PSE&G has acquired an industrial outdoor storage parcel in Hillside that will allow it to expand a substation on a neighboring property, brokers with NAI James E. Hanson said Wednesday.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Owner-occupier buys Middlesex flex building in $1 million deal arranged by Kislak
item.description: The Kislak Co. Inc. has brokered the sale of a nearly 6,400-square-foot industrial flex building in Middlesex Borough, the Woodbridge-based firm announced Wednesday.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Joshua Burd

item.title: Startup courier Gofo acquires Cirro E-commerce for access to retailers
item.description: Newbie parcel carrier Gofo stoked its big growth ambitions with the acquisition of Cirro E-commerce, an e-commerce logistics platform company, giving it better inroads to retail customers. The post Startup courier Gofo acquires Cirro E-commerce for access to retailers appeared first on FreightWaves.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Eric Kulisch

item.title: First Look: GXO Logistics Inc.
item.description: GXO's revenue increased to $13.2 billion in 2025 amid a confident outlook tied to outsourcing, e-commerce and automation trends. The post First Look: GXO Logistics Inc. appeared first on FreightWaves.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Noi Mahoney

item.title: KKR's Mortgage REIT Plans 'Year Of Transition' To Clean Up Portfolio
item.description: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust is planning to majorly restructure its portfolio this year. In what was described as a "year of transition" by CEO Matt Salem on KREF's Q4 earnings call, the mortgage REIT aims to sell off troubled assets on its internal...
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: kayla.carmicheal@bisnow.com

item.title: Logistics warehouse rents may rebound after 2025 drop: Prologis
item.description: The real estate giant is seeing prices begin to inflect in many markets as large retailers boost demand, CFO Tim Arndt said.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Max Garland

item.title: FedEx to deploy autonomous trailer unloader from Berkshire Grey
item.description: The logistics giant is currently in the final stage of piloting the technology, which is occurring at one facility with multiple unloaders, an executive said.
item.category: Relevant — Market Trends & Real Estate News
item.author: Max Garland

item.title: Gather AI raises $40m to scale its physical AI platform for global logistics
item.description: Warehouse operations can make or break an organization. That problem is at the heart of Gather AI’s latest funding round. The Pittsburgh-based startup announced it has raised $40 million in Series B financing, led by Smith Point Capital Management, the firm founded by former Salesforce co-CEO Keith Block. The raise brings Gather AI’s total funding […] The post Gather AI raises $40m to scale its physical AI platform for global logistics appeared first on FreightWaves.
item.category: Transactions — Property Sales & Leases
item.author: Mary O'Connell

item.title: Homebuilding Suppliers Flood Miami With Industrial Space For Sublease
item.description: Businesses that sell doors, windows and other building materials have suddenly found themselves with too much warehouse space amid a slowdown in home construction.

item.title: Leadership Lessons from Celeste Tanner, 2026 NAIOP Chair
item.description: Celeste Tanner, NAIOP's 2026 chair and co-founder, president, and chief development officer of Confluent Development, joined NAIOP’s Inside CRE podcast to share the leadership principles that have shaped her career in the collaborative world of commercial real estate.

item.title: The Shaw Group Expands Walker, Louisiana, Operations
item.description: Industrial engineering and fabrication company The Shaw Group plans to expand operations in Walker, Louisiana. The project is expected to create 209 jobs.
